title: Pleurolobus triflorus
intro: Pleurolobus triflorus, also known as Three-Flowered Snakeweed, is a shrubby plant native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico, belonging to the family Fabaceae. Traditionally, Pleurolobus triflorus is used to treat various health issues, including fever, sore throat, and respiratory problems, due to its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. In herbal medicine, Pleurolobus triflorus is commonly prepared as a tea or infusion, and also made into a tincture or syrup for the treatment of respiratory infections and other conditions.
taxonomy: Pleurolobus triflorus is classified within the family Fabaceae. This plant is a part of the subfamily Faboideae, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants. The genus Pleurolobus contains a single species, Pleurolobus triflorus. This species is a type of shrub or small tree that is native to tropical Africa. It is a member of the Legume family, which is characterized by its fruit that splits into two or more parts.
